reasoning: The Lean statement uses `∃!` over `ℕ × ℕ` with both components constrained to be positive (`p.1 > 0 ∧ p.2 > 0`), correctly encoding "unique positive integers $a, n$". The only potential concern is the natural-number subtraction in `p.1^(p.2+1) - (p.1+1)^(p.2) = 2001`, but since the RHS is 2001 > 0, any solution must satisfy $a^{n+1} > (a+1)^n$, which means natural subtraction agrees with integer subtraction there. So the set of solutions in ℕ matches the set of positive-integer solutions under the informal reading. The known unique solution (a=13, n=2) is captured either way since $13^3 - 14^2 = 2001$. The quantifier structure, typing, and conclusion all match the informal claim.
